name: Vineyards of Marmandais Loop
name_original: Parmi les vignobles du Marmandais — boucle
description: Cycle through the scenic Marmandais vineyards on this moderate 29.2-mile loop, exploring charming villages and river views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Meilhan-Sur-Garonne features a landscape shaped by the Garonne river and its network of canals, offering mostly flat to gently undulating terrain. The region is characterized by extensive vineyards, agricultural fields, and riverside paths. These routes often follow waterways, providing accessible touring cycling options for various skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Meilhan-Sur-Garonne The most popular touring cycling route is Vineyards of Marmandais Loop, a 29.2 miles (47.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through the local vineyard landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fontet Marina – Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Fontet, a moderate 22.1 miles (35.6 km) path. This route includes scenic sections along the Aqueduct of the Avance. Local touring cyclists also love the The Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Ship birth on the canal loop from Fontet, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through canal-side scenery, often completed in about 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Meilhan-Sur-Garonne is defined by canals, vineyards, and riverside paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes. The routes in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Meilhan-Sur-Garonne's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Meilhan-Sur-Garonne?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes in the Meilhan-Sur-Garonne area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region's landscape, shaped by the Garonne river and its canals, provides mostly flat to gently undulating terrain.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Meilhan-Sur-Garonne offers 38 easy touring cycling routes, many of which follow the flat, scenic canal and riverside paths. A great option for a relaxed ride is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41470328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Ship birth on the canal loop from Fontet&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 10 miles (16 km) long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne?
A: The terrain around Meilhan-Sur-Garonne is predominantly flat to gently undulating, making it ideal for touring cyclists. Routes often follow the Garonne river and its network of canals, passing through vineyards and agricultural fields. While there are some moderate climbs, significant elevation changes are rare.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the region?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, there are 17 difficult touring cycling routes available. These routes may feature longer distances or more varied terrain, offering a more strenuous experience while still allowing you to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: What are some popular circular touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295263756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Marmandais Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.2-mile (47.1 km) route that takes you through the local vineyard landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40952650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontet Marina – Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Fontet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne?
A: The routes often run alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2605415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views of barges and canal infrastructure like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassanne Lock&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also pass through extensive vineyards and agricultural fields. The region is rich in natural beauty and charming riverside villages.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne are typically spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer rainy days than winter. The vineyards are also particularly beautiful during these times.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by marinas or offer waterside views?
A: Absolutely. Given the region's focus on the Garonne river and its canals, many routes offer excellent waterside views and pass by marinas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourist barge on the canal – Fontet Marina loop from Fontet&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, easy route that highlights the canal and marina environment.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne?
A: The touring cycling routes in Meilhan-Sur-Garonne are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the peaceful canal-side paths, the scenic vineyard landscapes, and the generally flat and accessible terrain that makes for enjoyable rides.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to other towns or offer longer distances?
A: Yes, many routes along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne can be extended to connect to other towns or form longer journeys, as the canal path stretches for many kilometers. For a moderate distance, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41474061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontet Marina – The Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Montagoudin&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 30 km.
tips:
Cocumont, a funny name for a funny story: wise Benedictines from Conques hear the call of the cuckoo. Divine sign or sign of wine, they decide to build a church and plant new vines. This predestined place will be called Coculo Monté: Cuckoo Mountain! History or legend, one thing is certain, the 11th century church is still there! This little
chapel will quickly become the signature of the winegrowers of the Cave de
Cocumont, bottles, capsules and other labels will be systematically decorated with it.

In Meilhan-sur-Garonne, it is possible to see the loop of the Garonne and the Lateral Canal. The mound, a site registered and protected since October 29, 1987, allows walkers to enjoy an exceptional panorama over the entire Garonne valley. From the rocky peak, you can contemplate the meander of Dame Garonne which flirts with the Lateral Canal.

Built on the banks of the Garonne, as its name suggests, the small village lives to the rhythm of the numerous floods and the vagaries of the river. Every year, an international journalism festival takes place there, in a festive and family atmosphere. A good opportunity to discover the city, its beautiful church, to take a stroll on the water or to move your hips during the many concerts!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Among the vineyards of Marmandais — loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop is approximately 47 kilometers (29 miles) long. Komoot estimates a cycling time of around 2 hours and 50 minutes. However, given its moderate difficulty and the scenic nature of the route, which encourages stops, planning for a half-day to a full-day excursion is advisable to fully enjoy the vineyards, villages, and river views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a rolling to hilly terrain, typical of wine regions, with an elevation gain of approximately 309 meters (1,014 feet). You'll cycle through landscapes dominated by vineyards, which uniquely stretch across both the right and left banks of the Garonne River. Expect a mix of paved roads and possibly some well-maintained vineyard tracks.

faqs.question: Is the Among the vineyards of Marmandais — loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate difficulty, primarily due to its length and the cumulative elevation gain. While not overly technical, beginners should have a reasonable level of fitness and experience with longer rides and some ascents. It's a great option for those looking to challenge themselves beyond flat, easy routes.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are generally spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is usually pleasant, and the vineyards offer beautiful scenery—either budding green in spring or vibrant with harvest colors in autumn. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route on public roads and paths, dogs are generally permitted, especially if they are well-behaved and kept on a leash. If you plan to stop at any specific attractions or establishments along the way, it's always best to check their individual dog policies beforehand.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Among the vineyards of Marmandais — loop, as it utilizes public roads and pathways. You are free to explore the route at your leisure.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in the charming perched villages and historic bastides along the route, such as near Cocumont or Couthures-sur-Garonne Riverside Village. Look for public parking lots within these towns to start your ride.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a variety of sights. You'll pass through the heart of the Côtes du Marmandais vineyards, encounter charming villages, and enjoy views of the Garonne River. Notable highlights include Cocumont, the Canal de la Garonne at Meilhan-sur-Garonne, the picturesque Couthures-sur-Garonne Riverside Village, and the Aqueduct in Meilhan-sur-Garonne.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the EV3 France 42, Langon - Buzet-sur-Baïse, and Le Canal de Garonne à vélo : La Réole / Marmande. These intersections can offer opportunities to extend or modify your ride if you wish to explore further.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several villages and towns, you will find opportunities for refreshments. Places like Cocumont and Couthures-sur-Garonne Riverside Village are good spots to look for cafes, bakeries, or small restaurants to refuel during your ride.

faqs.question: What makes the vineyards of Marmandais unique on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The Côtes du Marmandais appellation is uniquely divided by the Garonne River, with vineyards on both its right and left banks. This geographical separation has historically led to distinct viticultural traditions and landscapes on each side, offering a varied and interesting experience as you cycle through different vineyard styles within the same region.

waypoints.name: Canal de la Garonne at Meilhan-sur-Garonne
